summary: The session focused on the Versailles-Washington System, detailing its establishment after World War I through the Treaty of Versailles and the Washington Naval Conference. The discussion highlighted the system's goals of promoting peace and stability, its eventual failure, and the consequences that led to World War II.
facts: The Treaty of Versailles was signed in 1919 to end the state of war between Germany and the Allied Powers. The Washington Naval Conference took place from 1921 to 1922 to establish agreements on naval disarmament among major naval powers. The Versailles-Washington System aimed to promote peace, stability, and international cooperation after World War I. The League of Nations was part of the principles underlying the Versailles-Washington System, emphasizing collective security. The harsh penalties and reparations imposed on Germany contributed to the rise of the Nazi dictatorship. The Great Depression of the 1930s weakened the global economy and the political order established after World War I. The failure of the Versailles-Washington System ultimately led to World War II.
